rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no session biography
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, NOT in curriculum body
Earn every technical term on first use
No narrating student behavior / no pat-on-back closers
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 87-88: 'because you kept noticing. The HTML looks different too, because you kept tightening' — narrates student actions instead of artifact agency
rules_evaluated.fix: Apply Sutherland-flip: move agency from student to artifact. Replace 'because you kept noticing' and 'because you kept tightening' with artifact-focused phrasing: 'The memory and HTML both tighten as misses surface and edits land.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No overclaim of student verification or coverage
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 85-92: claims 'the memory looks different from the one you left class with' as if the week of observation produces full transformation verification, but a week produces observation + sampling of edits, not full verification
rules_evaluated.fix: Scope claim to sample: 'The memory evolves as patterns surface. By end of week, sample three misses and trace what the memory lacked.' Or defer full-system claim to end of subsequent module.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 93: 'One glance per session for a week' — implied time-of-day anchor in 'glance' (morning observation habit)
rules_evaluated.fix: Replace 'One glance per session' with 'One observation per run' to make it time-neutral. Change line 93 to: 'Time: 35 minutes to set up. One observation run per day for a week.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in

rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 29, 45, 67: three **Prompt** labels missing destination chip *(Claude Code)* parenthetical
rules_evaluated.fix: Add *(Claude Code)* after each **Prompt** label: line 29 → '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*'; line 45 → '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*'; line 67 → '**Prompt** *(Claude Code, scheduled task)*' (note: line 67 correctly specifies the context already)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 32: '**+**' (bold in fence); lines 33-34: '**Settings → Connectors**' (bold in fence); lines 46-48: '**- Morning plan**' / '**- Daily risk scan**' / '**- Draft today's**' (bold bullet prefixes in fence)
rules_evaluated.fix: Remove all markdown bold/italic markup from inside the three fenced blocks. Lines 32-37 (fence 1), lines 47-55 (fence 2) — use plain text, bullets, or backticks for paths only. In fence 2, bullets become plain: '- Morning plan' not '**- Morning plan**'.

rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 50-52 (inside fence 2): three numbered questions [1. 2. 3.] listed in sequence. Prompt says 'ask one at a time' but format allows Claude to batch-post all three.
rules_evaluated.fix: Add explicit one-at-a-time structural enforcement inside the prompt. Replace lines 50-52 list with single-question phrasing: 'Ask me one question and wait for my answer before asking the next. Do not show me all three questions — ask, wait, then ask the next one.' Remove the numbered list or restructure as conditional: 'First: Which job — morning plan, daily risk scan, or next-move draft? Wait for my answer, then ask the second question.' (Alternative: keep list but prepend with 'Ask ONLY the first question now:' to enforce sequencing.)
